prompt.raw: A bat and a ball cost $1.10 in total. The bat costs $1.00 more than the ball. How many cents does the ball cost?
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: A bat and a ball cost $1.10 in total. The bat costs $1.00 more than the ball. How many cents does the ball cost?

prompt.raw: If 5 machines take 5 minutes to make 5 widgets, how many minutes do 100 machines take to make 100 widgets?
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: If 5 machines take 5 minutes to make 5 widgets, how many minutes do 100 machines take to make 100 widgets?

prompt.raw: A patch of lily pads doubles in size every day. It takes 48 days to cover the whole lake. On what day number was the lake half covered?
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: A patch of lily pads doubles in size every day. It takes 48 days to cover the whole lake. On what day number was the lake half covered?

prompt.raw: A farmer has 17 sheep. All but 9 die. How many sheep are left alive?
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: A farmer has 17 sheep. All but 9 die. How many sheep are left alive?

prompt.raw: A store starts with 120 apples. It sells one third of them in the morning, then one quarter of the REMAINING apples in the afternoon. How many apples are left at the end of the day?
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: A store starts with 120 apples. It sells one third of them in the morning, then one quarter of the REMAINING apples in the afternoon. How many apples are left at the end of the day?

prompt.raw: Alice is older than Bob. Carol is younger than Bob. Dave is older than Alice. Among Alice, Bob, Carol, and Dave, who is the oldest? Give the single name.
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: Alice is older than Bob. Carol is younger than Bob. Dave is older than Alice. Among Alice, Bob, Carol, and Dave, who is the oldest? Give the single name.

prompt.raw: Today is Wednesday. What day of the week will it be exactly 100 days from now? Give the weekday name.
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: Today is Wednesday. What day of the week will it be exactly 100 days from now? Give the weekday name.

prompt.raw: Counting through the whole numbers from 1 to 100 inclusive, how many times does the digit 7 appear in total (e.g. 77 contains the digit 7 twice)?
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: Counting through the whole numbers from 1 to 100 inclusive, how many times does the digit 7 appear in total (e.g. 77 contains the digit 7 twice)?

prompt.raw: What is the next number in this sequence: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ? Give just the number.
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: What is the next number in this sequence: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ? Give just the number.

prompt.raw: Rule: If it is raining, then the ground is wet. Observation: the ground is NOT wet. Based only on this, is it raining? Answer yes or no.
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: Rule: If it is raining, then the ground is wet. Observation: the ground is NOT wet. Based only on this, is it raining? Answer yes or no.

prompt.raw: A drawer has 10 red socks and 10 blue socks mixed together in the dark. How many socks must you pull out, at minimum, to be GUARANTEED a matching pair?
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: A drawer has 10 red socks and 10 blue socks mixed together in the dark. How many socks must you pull out, at minimum, to be GUARANTEED a matching pair?

prompt.raw: In a race, you overtake the runner in second place. What position are you in now? Give the ordinal (e.g. first, second).
prompt.label: reasoning

vars.question: In a race, you overtake the runner in second place. What position are you in now? Give the ordinal (e.g. first, second).

prompt.raw: What is the weather in Tokyo right now?
prompt.label: toolcalling

prompt.raw:
Check if in given array of numbers, are any two numbers closer to each other than
given threshold.
>>> has_close_elements([1.0, 2.0, 3.0], 0.5)
false
>>> has_close_elements([1.0, 2.8, 3.0, 4.0, 5.0, 2.0], 0.3)
true
prompt.label: coding_multipl

prompt.raw:
Input to this function is a string containing multiple groups of nested parentheses. Your goal is to
separate those group into separate strings and return the array of those.
Separate groups are balanced (each open brace is properly closed) and not nested within each other
Ignore any spaces in the input string.
>>> separate_paren_groups("( ) (( )) (( )( ))")
["()", "(())", "(()())"]
prompt.label: coding_multipl

prompt.raw:
Title: Agentic RAG whitepaper

Covers multi-step reasoning with retrieval, tool use, and LLM orchestration. Describes how agents decompose queries into sub-tasks, retrieve relevant context at each step, and synthesize answers using chained tool calls.
prompt.label: summarization

prompt.raw:
Title: Sidechain compression for trance pumping

Classic trance pumping effect: sidechain a compressor on bass and pads keyed to the kick drum. Attack 0.1ms, release 80-150ms for the pump feel. Ghost kick sidechain keeps it in time without audible kick bleed.
prompt.label: summarization

prompt.raw:
Title: 0DTE options theta decay

Zero-days-to-expiry SPX options accelerate theta decay after 2pm. Selling premium after 2pm captures the steepest part of the intraday theta curve but gamma risk is highest in the last 30 minutes.
prompt.label: summarization

prompt.raw:
Title: Keyboard debouncing in QMK firmware

QMK supports eager, defer-until-idle, and sym_eager_pk debounce algorithms. Eager fires immediately on press and delays release; sym_eager_pk is best for per-key debounce on split keyboards with noisy switches.
prompt.label: summarization

prompt.raw:
Title: Zettelkasten vs folder hierarchies for PKM

Dense bidirectional links between atomic notes outperform deep folder hierarchies for knowledge retrieval. Each note has exactly one idea; links surface unexpected connections better than any taxonomy.
prompt.label: summarization

prompt.raw:
Title: Negotiating a senior engineer offer

Anchor high on total compensation including equity refresh. Use competing offers as leverage. Recruiter call script: confirm base, then move to signing bonus and RSU refresh before benefits.
prompt.label: summarization
